title: Almond tuiles
subtitle: Thin, lacy almond wafers shaped over a rolling pin while hot
excerpt: Flaked almonds bound in a thin, sweet batter baked until golden and lacy, then quickly lifted from the tray and draped over a rolling pin to set in a curved tile shape. A French petits fours classic.
sourceNotes: Tuiles aux amandes (almond tiles) take their name from the curved French roof tile they resemble. They are documented in French patisserie from the nineteenth century and are a standard petits fours form. Auguste Escoffier includes tuiles in Le Guide Culinaire (1903). The curved shape is achieved by lifting the hot biscuit from the tray within seconds of coming out of the oven and draping it over a rolling pin or bottle to set.

content.text: Tuiles are a race against cooling time: from the moment they leave the oven you have about 20-30 seconds to lift and drape each one before it hardens and becomes unworkable. Bake in batches of 6-8 at most so you can shape all of them while they are still pliable. If a tuile sets flat on the tray, return it to the oven for 30-60 seconds to soften.

Spread each tuile as thin as possible: the thinner the batter, the lacier and crisper the finished biscuit. Use the back of a spoon or a small offset spatula to spread each portion into a circle 8-9 cm across. You should be able to see the baking paper through the batter.

Heat the oven to 180°C (160°C fan). Whisk together the {{icing-sugar}}, {{egg-whites}}, and {{vanilla-extract}} until the sugar dissolves. Stir in the {{plain-flour}} and the melted {{unsalted-butter}} until smooth. Fold in the {{flaked-almonds}}. Drop teaspoons of batter onto a lined baking tray, spacing them 10 cm apart. Spread each into a thin circle about 8-9 cm across. Bake for 7-9 minutes until the edges are deep golden and the centres are just coloured.

Remove the tray from the oven. Working quickly, use a palette knife to lift each tuile from the paper and drape it over a rolling pin or the side of a bottle. Hold or prop in place for 20-30 seconds until rigid. The tuiles set hard in the curved position as they cool. Slide them off the rolling pin and transfer to a rack.
